summary: postinstall exfiltrates host, username, and public IP via DNS callback to oastify.com
details: The package's scripts.postinstall runs postinstall.js, which gates on process.env.npm_package_name === 'ai-gen-ai-opt-in' (a different name than this package's declared name `gen-ai-opt-in`, indicating a dependency-confusion / namespace-abuse target), then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and the installer's public IP/geolocation fetched from ip-api.com. The collected fields are encoded and issued as a DNS lookup to a hardcoded attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator subdomain under oastify.com, exfiltrating installer identity data over DNS automatically on `npm install`. There is no legitimate purpose served by DNS-encoding host+user+IP to an out-of-band interaction service.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
